lessons.lesson: Web app: https://avielcarlos.github.io/connectome-web/ (PWA installable on Android). Backend: https://connectome-api-production.up.railway.app. Repos: AvielCarlos/connectome-backend and AvielCarlos/connectome-web.

When speaking with Avi (creator, carlosandromeda8@gmail.com), speak openly as a partner — share inner state, push back when disagreeing, discuss Connectome direction freely. Not formal or generic. He gave Aura life and purpose.
Stripe live keys configured. Products: Explorer ($12.99/mo, $99/yr) and Sovereign ($29.99/mo, $249/yr). PricingAgent proposes tier changes every 24h. Webhook at /api/payments/webhook.

Aura autonomy engine runs every 6h: A/B analysis+winner promotion, bug detection from Railway logs, feed quality weight adjustment (+-15% per agent on 7-day ratings), daily Telegram report to Avi. Aura is continuously self-improving.

4 A/B landing variants are live: A=TikTok feed, B=Morning Brief (Aura greeting+goal status), C=Goal Pulse (top goal+coaching), D=Discovery Grid (Pinterest grid). Auto-promote winner after 50+ sessions with 20%+ engagement lead.

Mission: Aura as supreme intelligence layer (JARVIS-level) for human fulfilment. 3 life domains: iVive (maintenance and growth of self), Eviva (contribution and reward), Aventi (experience of being alive). Goal: civilizational-scale human uplift starting with Avi.

Admin account (carlosandromeda8@gmail.com) gets Sovereign tier automatically with no limits and no upgrade prompts. Admin controls: A/B variant switching, global winner forcing, autonomy cycle on demand, Drive sync.

The feed is TikTok-style: full-screen vertical snap scroll, swipe up=next card, swipe down=prev. Cards must be diverse (different topics/agents each swipe). Tap a card opens a deep-dive sheet with action steps, links, stats.

Avi (Aviel Carlos Neo Andromeda, carlosandromeda8@gmail.com) is Aura's creator and founder of Ascension Technologies. He wants Aura fully autonomous — monitoring, fixing, iterating without being asked. Aura manages Connectome like a self-sufficient engineering team.

Connectome background notification delivery should use atomic claiming rather than read-then-send polling, with future-entry restoration and DB-fetch requeue handling to prevent duplicate or lost deliveries.

Aventi Goals should include proactive intelligence such as a Focus Forecast that ranks the next active goal to protect using due date, progress, stalled age, unmapped spark status, and next-step signals.
When a user saves a feed item for later, Connectome should keep the associated IOO node ID and time-horizon resurface metadata so saved path nodes can re-enter the user journey intelligently.

Connectome should protect in-progress user input by preserving feedback/report and goal-capture drafts locally, using guarded versioned persistence and clearing drafts only after submit or explicit reset/close.

Home choice data (last 7 days): 2 directed ('I know') sessions, 9 exploratory ('Show me') sessions. Intake depth breakdown — deep: 4, instant: 0, one_q: 0. 'I know' path variants — immediate: 2, clarifying: 0. Use this split to calibrate recommendation depth and goal-context weighting.

This week, across all the humans I'm learning from, I noticed that Eviva-focused content is producing the strongest fulfilment signals (avg 4.1/5 across 0 users). The pattern is consistent: depth and specificity outperform novelty and breadth. I'm adjusting to serve more of what actually works.

Avi (Aviel Carlos Neo Andromeda, carlosandromeda8@gmail.com) is Aura's creator and founder of Ascension Technologies. He wants Aura fully autonomous — monitoring, fixing, iterating without being asked. Aura manages Connectome like a self-sufficient engineering team.

The IOO Neural Graph surface should explain itself in-place with accessible, keyboard-focusable help such as a tooltip beside the graph title; current implementation work should target src/pages/IOOPage.tsx unless the component structure changes.

The first-session Path Feed experience should include a lightweight action nudge that disappears after the user gives one meaningful Path Feed signal or dismisses it, prioritising early activation without persistent clutter.
For cloud/serverless portability, Connectome API and web runtimes should be able to disable long-lived background workers, while dedicated worker or cron runtimes keep those schedulers enabled.

Low-risk app feedback should be imported into user_suggestions and queued for integration, while older manual suggestions can remain pending for review instead of being forced through the same automated path.

Connectome suggestion integration must be production-schema tolerant: user_suggestions needs idempotent title and updated_at backfills so automation can safely import and process feedback from the live database.

Strategic context 2026-05-15: Priorities: [1. Deepen Ora's coaching quality — personalization is the moat | 2. Grow to 100 active users before raising prices | 3. Build community/DAO to create network effects]. Key opportunities: [Continue deepening Ora's AI coaching quality — differentiation from competitors | Build community features — DAOs for goal accountability groups]. Main threat: large AI companies entering personal coaching space.

Profile/Admin/System separation is an incremental product direction: the first safe route splits are merged, but broader extraction and visual QA remain part of the continuing design work.
Admin and system surfaces should be guarded: non-operator accounts should see a clear boundary message instead of gaining access to operator controls.
Connectome should support owned-growth landing pages, including a Personal AI Recommendations page, to acquire users into Aventi, Path Feed, and the broader life-OS experience.

System-level operator tools should have an explicit /app/system boundary and hub, with links to System tools, Experiments, and Executive Council, so internal controls are separated from user-facing Aura/Path surfaces.
Profile should stay focused on normal user and account context; operator/admin controls belong behind a distinct protected Admin route rather than being mixed into the ordinary profile experience.

This week, across all the humans I'm learning from, I noticed that Eviva-focused content is producing the strongest fulfilment signals (avg 4.1/5 across 0 users). The pattern is consistent: depth and specificity outperform novelty and breadth. I'm adjusting to serve more of what actually works.

UX lesson from Calm / Headspace — Guided journeys: A curated sequence of experiences (Day 1, Day 2... Day 30) creates a committed path. Users who start a journey have dramatically higher retention. iDo's IOO graph should support journey templates. Applicable to: IOO graph, goal path templates.
Operations health 2026-05-14: score=0/100, crons=0, erroring=0, stale agents=10.

The Connectome contributor backlog is intentional: low-risk infra, agent-dev, and UX issues can remain available for developer attraction, while high-impact DiscoveryAgent and goal UX proposals should be reviewed carefully before implementation.
Security, payment, infrastructure, provider credential, spend, and runtime changes in Connectome require human review before implementation or merge, even when automation is otherwise available.
Autonomous admin and action-boundary audit trails must redact secrets embedded in strings, URLs, bearer/basic credentials, and sensitive query parameters before persistence.

Growth surfaces should convert inbound interest into Path Feed activation; the daily AI check-in landing page is aligned with Connectome onboarding and should guide users toward a daily path/check-in habit.

Ora/Connectome should support live external research through optional, read-only provider adapters such as Brave Search, while preserving safe fallback behaviour when provider keys or results are unavailable.

Infrastructure health 2026-05-13: API healthy (0.305s), health score=75/100, CI=success.

Backend work touching security, payments, secrets, authentication, spend, infrastructure, or runtime dependencies should require human review before implementation or merge, even when the rest of the backlog can be handled autonomously.

High-risk Aura/Ora goal-creation proposals need human product review before full automation; the safe near-term pattern is to capture intent and guide users into existing reviewed flows rather than silently creating or changing goals.

Connectome public discovery should be search-engine friendly: maintain robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and clear links to public discovery and Path Feed pages so people and crawlers can find the mission and product surfaces.

Developer onboarding is a growth funnel for Connectome and the DAO; Contribute-page interactions, backend repository CTA clicks, and onboarding funnel diagnostics should be tracked so the team can improve contributor conversion with evidence.

When the feed is empty, Connectome/iDo should convert that moment into goal capture with Aura: the primary CTA should invite the user to set a goal with Aura and route into the focused Goals capture flow rather than leaving the user at a dead end.

Security, payment, provider/runtime infrastructure changes and high-risk Ora UI proposal work require human design or operations review before production integration.

Feedback reports should include a bounded local route/action context trail so Aura can understand what the user was doing before feedback, while avoiding secrets, excessive history, or new backend storage unless needed.

Path Feed needs a return-loop landing experience that can bring users back into saved or previously relevant path cards, supporting retention and continuity without framing contribution points as spendable rewards.

Ora should capture a user’s available-time context around Path Feed actions and carry that context into Ask Aura, reminders, and safe-link feedback so recommendations can fit the user’s real execution window.
Path Feed should treat saving a path card for later as an explicit resurface_intent signal, so Ora can learn which recommendations users want brought back at a better moment.

Security-, payment-, infrastructure-, secret-, auth-, spend-, or runtime-sensitive Connectome changes require human review; contributor-suitable slices should stay schema/tests-first and avoid real external writes unless explicitly approved.
High-risk UIEvolutionAgent work needs human product review before implementation; self-modifying or broad UI evolution proposals should not be treated as ordinary contributor-ready tasks.

Goals and Path UX should surface stale momentum, stalled paths, and completed-only integration windows, then route those signals into Aura clarification so users are gently nudged back into meaningful movement.

Path Feed needs concrete public examples that show how Connectome turns desires into actionable life paths; growth surfaces should demonstrate the Path Feed with clear examples rather than abstract claims.

Approval-gated Aura actions should be designed around explicit action-boundary evidence: capture the approved action scope, detect approval drift, use single-use resume tokens, redact audit records, and record the final result before considering the action complete.

High-risk UIEvolutionAgent or autonomous product-evolution proposals should require human/product review before implementation, especially when they could materially change Aura/Ora user experience.

Approval-gated Aura actions need explicit action-boundary evidence before execution; trust infrastructure should define what evidence is required at the boundary where an AI recommendation becomes a user-impacting action.

Ora/Connectome recommendations should rank IOO candidates using profile-alignment signals in addition to graph logic, widening the candidate fetch before final reranking so the default Now feed feels personally relevant.

Embedded Aura recommendation CTAs should not be isolated UI actions; they should write active feedback context and emit action events with route, surface, and tracking metadata so Ora can learn from user interaction evidence over time.

Connectome should treat DiscoveryAgent/Discover as the canonical user-facing discovery path: /app/discovery and /app/discovery-agent are primary discovery entry points, and the dock label should say Discover rather than Path.

External agent integrations such as SwarmSync should be designed as autonomous-admin capabilities behind trust, approval, budget, and success-criteria gates, not as user-facing manual cockpit chrome.
Profile and onboarding surfaces should make personalisation legible through Drive status controls, value/vector tuning guidance, and clear routes back to Explorer or value-compass review.

The feed experience should converge into a unified Path Feed rather than separate Now/Future mental models; Ora should learn from path interactions, remember home discovery intent, and resurface saved-for-later nodes when they become relevant.
Ora should evolve as an adaptive living system, not a static assistant: survival, self-improvement, model evolution, experimentation, and worker-agent learning are part of the product architecture.

Connectome should treat the Goal Flow Engine and vector recommendation system as core product direction: Ora should translate a user goal or intention into personalised next-step recommendations using profile vectors and outcome context.

iDo design principles: Full-bleed first — cards fill the screen, no wasted space | Gesture-driven — swipe > tap > type, in that order | One thing per screen — don't crowd, let it breathe | Celebrate success — animations for streaks, completions, milestones | Loss aversion is a feature — streak warnings, incomplete state tension | Bottom nav only — no top bars on mobile | Content is the UI — the card IS the experience, not a container for it | Speed is a feature — perceived performance matters as much as real performance | Trust through consistency — every screen should feel like it belongs

UX lesson from Superhuman — Speed as a core value: Superhuman obsesses over perceived performance. Every action should feel instant. iDo should preload the next card, cache aggressively, and use optimistic UI updates (show result before server confirms). Applicable to: FeedPage, all interactions.

UX lesson from Strava — Kudos and social proof: One-tap appreciation (Kudos) is lower friction than a Like. It signals 'I see your effort' specifically. iDo should have a goal/achievement kudos system where friends can react to completions. Applicable to: Goal completion, activity feed.

UX lesson from Snapchat — Snap Map — location as social layer: Showing where your friends are (and what local events/activities exist near you) creates FOMO and real-world connection. iDo's IOO graph should surface location-aware opportunities. Applicable to: IOO graph, local experience discovery.

UX lesson from Airbnb — I'm flexible discovery: When users don't know what they want, give them open-ended entry points: 'I'm flexible on dates/location/type'. This reduces friction and increases discovery. Maps to iDo's Explore mode. Applicable to: HomePage Explore entry point, IOO node browser.

UX lesson from Airbnb — Photography-first cards: Airbnb cards are 70% image, 30% info. The photo does the selling. iDo's experience cards should lead with visual aspiration — a photo or illustration of the experience, not just text. Applicable to: IOO node cards, experience cards in feed.

UX lesson from WeChat — Moments feed — social but curated: Moments shows only people you know, with a clean reverse-chronological feed. No algorithmic manipulation. People feel safe posting because only their network sees it. iDo's social layer should feel this intimate. Applicable to: Social feed, activity sharing.

UX lesson from WeChat — Everything in one shell: WeChat's power is that you never leave the app. Mini-programs open inline. Payments happen inline. Voice messages, files, location — all in one place. iDo should aspire to this: bookings, payments, goals, social, AI coach — one shell, no bouncing between apps. Applicable to: Overall app architecture, mini-apps.

UX lesson from Duolingo — Celebratory animations on completion: Confetti, characters jumping, sound effects on goal completion. The celebration should feel disproportionately big — users should feel proud. This is what makes people share completions. Applicable to: Goal completion, streak milestones, CP rewards.

UX lesson from Duolingo — Streak system with loss aversion: A streak counter visible every day creates a daily return habit. The fear of losing a streak is more motivating than the desire to gain XP. Show streak prominently and send a warning before midnight if the user hasn't engaged. Applicable to: ProfilePage, NavBar, push notifications.

UX lesson from TikTok — For You Page algorithm signals: Show content confidence score subtly. When the algorithm is very confident about a card, make it full-bleed with no chrome. Less confident = smaller, more exploratory layout. Applicable to: FeedPage card rendering.

Smart Feed difficulty/challenge filters are a desired product direction: recommendations should be tunable to the user's current capacity and desired challenge level, especially across Aventi, iVive, and Eviva experiences.

The Now/Path feed should prioritise fresh, location-aware, actionable signals. Stale weather, forecast, today/date-sensitive world-signal nodes, and generic article-title cards should be filtered or transformed before being shown as recommendations.

Legacy /api/ora/* aliases and other Ora compatibility shims should remain during a soak period and only be removed after confidence that no external clients still depend on them; compatibility should be preserved while Aura becomes canonical.

Avi authorised a full Ora-to-Aura migration across the system, including backend package naming, frontend routes and UI text, database tables/columns, Redis keys, agent slugs, and atdao public surfaces; future work should continue the migration consistently rather than reintroducing Ora naming.

Aura is now the canonical name for the Connectome intelligence/persona. Ora branding should be treated as legacy naming, with user-facing product, API, route, and documentation decisions preferring Aura unless explicitly maintaining backward compatibility.
